tool_args.label: DPR offered unilateral price concession
tool_args.raw_text: DPR originally proposed $8,604,427 for this extension. Through discussions, DPR offered a unilateral price concession resulting in a final estimated expenditure amount of $7,849,087

payload.claim_text: DPR offered a unilateral price concession after discussions, reducing the proposed amount from $8,604,427 to $7,849,087.
payload.subject: Contract 6%19338 extension pricing

evidence_refs.source_quote: DPR originally proposed $8,604,427 for this extension. Through discussions, DPR offered a unilateral price concession resulting in a final estimated expenditure amount of $7,849,087 over the extension period. As a result, cost avoidance of approximately $755,340 were achieved. The pricing for the extension has been found fair and reasonable.

payload.subject: Contract 6%19338

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC Transit requests MTA Board approval of a three-year extension to the contract for Ultrasonic Internal Rail Flaw Inspections.

tool_args.label: Contract awarded as noncompetitive sole source
tool_args.raw_text: The contract was awarded pursuant to EO168 to DPR Ultrasonic Technologies, LLC ("DPR") on November 1, 2017, and ratified by the December 2017 Board for a period of five years (through October 31, 2022), in the total estimated amount of $10,352,334, as a noncompetitive estimated quantity contract due to the specific ultrasonic testing performed to ensure track integrity

payload.claim_text: The contract was awarded as a noncompetitive estimated quantity contract pursuant to EO168 because DPR is the only firm that can operate its equipment for ultrasonic internal rail flaw inspections.
payload.subject: Contract 6%19338

evidence_refs.source_quote: The contract was awarded pursuant to EO168 to DPR Ultrasonic Technologies, LLC

tool_args.label: Ultrasonic testing methodology
tool_args.raw_text: Under this contract, DPR performs ultrasonic testing of rail-using equipment of its own design mounted to NYC Transit-owned and operated Track Geometry Cars ("TGCs"). Ultrasonic inspection is a function of ensuring track integrity by detecting potential flaws in rail that may not be visible to the naked eye by emitting high-frequency soundwaves into the rail and subsequently measuring how those soundwaves move through the rail.

payload.claim_text: DPR performs ultrasonic testing of rail using equipment of its own design mounted to NYC Transit-owned and operated Track Geometry Cars, detecting flaws in rail by emitting high-frequency soundwaves and measuring how they move through the rail.
payload.subject: Contract 6%19338

evidence_refs.source_quote: DPR performs ultrasonic testing of rail-using equipment of its own design mounted to NYC Transit-owned and operated Track Geometry Cars

tool_args.label: Continuous inspection minimizes impact
tool_args.raw_text: The inspection process is continuous, enabling the detection of flaws without requiring the TGC vehicle to stop, minimizing its impact to revenue service operations.

payload.claim_text: The ultrasonic inspection process is continuous, enabling flaw detection without requiring the Track Geometry Car to stop, minimizing impact to revenue service operations.
payload.subject: Contract 6%19338

evidence_refs.source_quote: The inspection process is continuous, enabling the detection of flaws without requiring the TGC vehicle to stop, minimizing its impact to revenue service operations.
